name: Using a ND Filter for Long Exposure Photography | A Guide

step.name: Set your composition and shot up as normal, taking time to frame the shot car...
step.text: Set your composition and shot up as normal, taking time to frame the shot carefully, set aperture according to the depth of field you want and the focus point to achieve the depth of focus.

step.name: Take your initial test shot and check the histogram for exposure - use exposu...
step.text: Take your initial test shot and check the histogram for exposure - use exposure compensation (AV) to make fine tune adjustments up or down to ensure you capture the best exposure possible.

step.name: Now make a note of the aperture (F number) and shutter speed that the camera ...
step.text: Now make a note of the aperture (F number) and shutter speed that the camera has metered with your exposure compensation added into the equation.

step.name: The camera exposure modes, aperture and shutter priority (A or S) can only ex...
step.text: The camera exposure modes, aperture and shutter priority (A or S) can only expose for a maximum of 30 seconds so you may need to switch to manual exposure mode (M) on your dial.

step.name: Set aperture to the same as you had in step 2 and change shutter speed to BUL...
step.text: Set aperture to the same as you had in step 2 and change shutter speed to BULB mode.

step.name: You can either calculate in your head the exposure time required or use a sma...
step.text: You can either calculate in your head the exposure time required or use a smartphone app to provide you the extended exposure time based on the density of the filter you are using. example: normal shot without ND filter - shutter speed = 0.25 @ F16

step.name: Using a 6 stop ND means the exposure is extended to 1 stop - 1/2
step.text: Using a 6 stop ND means the exposure is extended to 1 stop - 1/2

step.name: Smartphone apps - just type in ND on the apps store and there are several app...
step.text: Smartphone apps - just type in ND on the apps store and there are several apps that you can use that will allow you to input the standard exposure time and convert the required exposure time based on the filters you are planning to use be it - 1 stop, 6 stop or 10 stop or any combination of filters you want to stack together and try.

step.name: If the required exposure time with the ND density you have chosen is greater ...
step.text: If the required exposure time with the ND density you have chosen is greater than 30sec then you need to switch to Manual exposure and set the shutter speed to BULB

step.name: Before you insert/attach the ND filter set the camera's focus to manual focus...
step.text: Before you insert/attach the ND filter set the camera's focus to manual focus, making sure you have already focused.

step.name: Now insert/attach your ND filter and using a remote release shutter release a...
step.text: Now insert/attach your ND filter and using a remote release shutter release and timer (your phone will have one) expose the shot for the required time calculated in step 6.
